content.sanskrit: सर्वं खल्विदं ब्रह्म तज्जलानिति शान्त उपासीत ।
content.transliteration: sarvaṁ khalvidaṁ brahma tajjalāniti śānta upāsīta |
content.text: All this is Brahman. From Him all things originate, into Him all things dissolve, and in Him they breathe — meditate on that calmly.
In the beginning, O dear one, this was Existence (Sat) alone, one only, without a second.

content.sanskrit: स य एषोऽणिमैतदात्म्यमिदं सर्वं तत्सत्यं स आत्मा तत्त्वमसि श्वेतकेतो ॥
content.transliteration: sa ya eṣo'ṇimaitadātmyamidaṁ sarvaṁ tatsatyaṁ sa ātmā tattvamasi śvetaketo ||
content.text: That which is the finest essence — in It all that exists has its self. That is the Truth. That is the Self. And that, O Shvetaketu, is what you are. (Tat Tvam Asi)

content.sanskrit: अध्यापयस्व भगव इति होपससाद सनत्कुमारं नारदः ।
content.transliteration: adhyāpayasva bhagava iti hopasasāda sanatkumāraṁ nāradaḥ |
content.text: Narada approached Sanatkumara and said: 'Teach me, O Revered One.'

content.sanskrit: यो वै भूमा तत्सुखं नाल्पे सुखमस्ति भूमैव सुखम् ।
content.transliteration: yo vai bhūmā tatsukhaṁ nālpe sukhamasti bhūmaiva sukham |
content.text: That which is infinite (Bhuma) is happiness. There is no happiness in the finite. Only in the Infinite is there happiness.

content.sanskrit: अथ यदिदमस्मिन् ब्रह्मपुरे दहरं पुण्डरीकं वेश्म दहरोऽस्मिन्नन्तराकाशस्तस्मिन्नेतदन्वेष्टव्यं तद्वाव विजिज्ञासितव्यम् ।
content.transliteration: atha yadidamasmin brahma pure daharaṁ puṇḍarīkaṁ veśma daharo'sminnantarākāśastasminnetadanveṣṭavyaṁ tadvāva vijijñāsitavyam |
content.text: Now, in this city of Brahman (the body) there is a small lotus-shaped dwelling. Within it is a small inner space. That which is within that should be sought; that should indeed be known.
